description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly dark gray surface. Patches of rusty brown coloration are scattered across its exterior, suggesting the presence of iron oxide or weathering effects. The stone's surface is marked by shallow grooves and subtle scratches, giving it a natural, worn appearance. Some areas display a slight sheen, while others are matte, indicating variable surface texture. The overall mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of solid rock, likely granite or basalt. Its size and form make it suitable as an urban obstacle or decorative element in parks or along sidewalks.
description: A rugged, dark gray stone with rusty brown patches and a rough, scratched surface, showing signs of natural weathering.
description_view_0: This view highlights the broadest face of the stone, with a large rusty brown patch dominating the upper surface and visible shallow scratches.
description_view_1: This angle shows more of the stone's angular edges and a smaller brown patch, with the surface appearing slightly more matte and less scratched.
description_view_2: The stone appears darker and more uniform from this side, with a single brown patch and pronounced angularity on the lower edge.
description_view_3: This view reveals a mix of brown and gray areas, with the surface texture appearing rough and the stone's irregular shape more evident.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside verge, or landscaped sidewalk
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with rusty brown patches and shallow linear scratches, giving a natural, weathered appearance.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible surface scratches and patches of discoloration, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
